title: centaurea setabensis subsp. humilis
intro: Centaurea setabensis subsp. humilis, also known as a sub-species of the knapweed, is a flowering plant belonging to the sunflower family. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits include relief from pain, reducing fever, and treating various skin conditions, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antitumor activities. Centaurea setabensis subsp. humilis can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal uses, although its efficacy and safety may vary depending on the preparation method and individual tolerance.
